title: gagea maroccana
intro: Gagea maroccana, also known as the Moroccan gagea or yellow star of Bethlehem, is a species of flowering plant in the lily family that is native to the Atlas Mountains in Morocco. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Gagea maroccana is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as being used as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Gagea maroccana are typically made by infusing the plant in water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or salves that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
